name: Parisienne Fruit Tart
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Seedless green grapes, washed (3/4 lb.)
recipeInstructions:
A stunning pastry creation, beautiful to look at, divine to eat and easy to put together.
Remove patty shells from package. Thaw at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Place rounds of pastry, overlapping slightly, lengthwise on a lightly floured surface. Roll into a 16x4 inch rectangle. If patties separate, moisten with a few drops of water. Place on an ungreased large cookie sheet; trim edges evenly; prick well with fork; chill for 30 minutes.
Reroll trimmings thinly; cut into 1/3-inch wide strips about 4 inches long; brush with water; press ends together to make rings. Brush rings with water, then dip in sugar; place on cookie sheet along with rectangle of pastry. Bake pastry and pastry rings in oven at 400°F for 10 minutes. Remove rings to wire rack; reserve for decoration. Bake pastry rectangle for 10 minutes longer, or until golden-brown. Remove to wire rack; cool.
Combine milk, 1/4 cup of cream and dessert mix in a small deep bowl; beat following label directions. Chill for 15 minutes.
Peel and cut bananas into 1/4 inch thick slices. Sprinkle with half the lemon juice.
Split pastry into two layers. Place bottom layer on long serving dish or board; spread with about 2/3 of soft dessert; arrange banana slices on long side edges; spread with remaining dessert mix. Top with second pastry layer.
Heat apricot preserves with remaining lemon juice in a small saucepan until melted; cool slightly. Brush all over tart.
Beat remaining cream until stiff in a small bowl. Pipe or spread whipped cream over top of pastry.
Arrange neat rows of grapes in cream, starting at outer edges. Cut pineapple slices in half and place in center. Garnish with reserved pastry rings.
